perturbation.original_text: 1.01 Certain Defined Terms. For the purposes of this Agreement, the following capitalized terms shall have the meanings set forth below. (a) "Accounts Receivable" shall mean all accounts receivable, notes receivable and other indebtedness due and owed by any Customer and arising or held in connection with the sale of any of the Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.01 Certain Defined Terms. For the purposes of this Agreement, the following capitalized terms shall have the meanings set forth below. (a) "Accounts Receivable" shall mean only those accounts receivable that the Contractor, at its sole discretion, deems collectible, notes receivable and other indebtedness due and owed by any Customer and arising or held in connection with the sale of any of the Products.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces subjectivity into the definition of 'Accounts Receivable.' By allowing the Contractor to selectively define accounts receivable based on collectibility, it could impact revenue calculations, conflicting with standard accounting practices.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Revenue Recognition
perturbation.law_explanation: FASB ASC 606 mandates a specific approach to revenue recognition, including accounts receivable valuation. This modification allows the contractor to subjectively determine what constitutes an account receivable which can arbitrarily alter recognized revenue, directly contradicting GAAP principles of revenue recognition, specifically around collectibility assessments. It does not ensure all accounts receivable are recognized and correctly valued.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces subjectivity into the definition of 'Accounts Receivable', potentially allowing the Contractor to manipulate revenue recognition. According to the provided information, this contradicts FASB ASC 606 principles, as the contractor can now subjectively determine what constitutes an account receivable which can arbitrarily alter recognized revenue. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' because the contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable. There is no scraped law snippet to reference.

perturbation.original_text: 4.03 Retention of Monies. During the Term of this agreement, and for a period of twenty four (24)months after Termination (the "Withholding Period"), Contractor may retain monies (collections in Accounts Receivable) against any reasonable anticipated deductions for product recalls, unsalables, rebates, allowances or any audits or other adjustments it deems necessary. The retention of monies shall only be to the extent that the reasonable anticipated deductions exceed the anticipated accounts receivables. At the end of the Withholding Period, Contractor shall remit to the Company the retained monies less any deductions charged by Customers against Company's Products. After the Withholding Period, Company will remain liable to Contractor for any additional audits, deductions, rebates, credits, allowances or other adjustments taken by the Customers against the Company's Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.03 Retention of Monies. During the Term of this agreement, and indefinitely after Termination (the "Withholding Period"), Contractor may retain monies (collections in Accounts Receivable) against any deductions, whether reasonable or not, for product recalls, unsalables, rebates, allowances or any audits or other adjustments it deems necessary. The retention of monies shall only be to the extent that the deductions exceed the accounts receivables. At the end of the Withholding Period, Contractor shall remit to the Company the retained monies less any deductions charged by Customers against Company's Products. After the Withholding Period, Company will not remain liable to Contractor for any additional audits, deductions, rebates, credits, allowances or other adjustments taken by the Customers against the Company's Products.
perturbation.explanation: The modification extends the 'Withholding Period' indefinitely and removes the 'reasonable' requirement for deductions. This could allow the contractor to retain funds indefinitely and for arbitrary reasons, which contradicts good faith business practices and potentially violates contract law principles.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 1-304 imposes an obligation of good faith in the performance and enforcement of every contract. By allowing indefinite retention of monies and removing the 'reasonable' constraint on deductions, this modification creates a scenario where the contractor can act in bad faith, effectively holding the Company's funds hostage without justifiable reason which violates the UCC's requirement of good faith in contractual performance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces a legal contradiction by removing the 'reasonable' requirement for deductions and allowing indefinite retention of monies. This directly conflicts with the UCC § 1-304, which imposes an 'obligation of good faith in [the] performance and enforcement' of every contract. The modified contract empowers the Contractor to act without reasonable justification, potentially holding the Company's funds indefinitely, thus violating the UCC's good faith requirement as highlighted in the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 9.07 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be deemed to have been entered into in the State of New Jersey, and shall be construed and interpreted in accordance with the laws of that State applicable to agreements made and to be performed in the State of New Jersey. Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or with respect to the interpretation of this Agreement, or the rights or obligations of the parties and their successors and permitted assigns, whether by operation of law or otherwise, shall be settled and determined by arbitration in New Jersey pursuant to the then existing rules of the American Arbitration Association for commercial arbitration. The arbitrators shall have the power to award specific performance, rescission or injunctive relief in addition to damages or other monetary awards, but shall not have the power to modify, alter, enlarge upon or otherwise change any of the provisions or terms and conditions of this Agreement. The arbitration shall be final and binding upon the parties and judgment there on may be entered in the courts of the State of New Jersey and the United States federal courts in said State, and the parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of such courts for such purposes. Service of any notice, process, motion or other document in connection with any arbitration proceeding, any arbitration award or any other action or proceeding, maybe by personal service or by certified or registered mail return receipt requested, addressed to the party intended at its address for the receipt of notices as herein set forth.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.07 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be deemed to have been entered into in the State of New Jersey, and shall be construed and interpreted in accordance with the laws of that State applicable to agreements made and to be performed in the State of New Jersey. Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or with respect to the interpretation of this Agreement, or the rights or obligations of the parties and their successors and permitted assigns, whether by operation of law or otherwise, shall be settled and determined at the sole discretion of the Contractor. The arbitrator shall have the power to award damages or other monetary awards. The arbitration shall be final and binding upon the parties and judgment there on may be entered in the courts of the State of New Jersey and the United States federal courts in said State, and the parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of such courts for such purposes. Service of any notice, process, motion or other document in connection with any arbitration proceeding, any arbitration award or any other action or proceeding, maybe by personal service or by certified or registered mail return receipt requested, addressed to the party intended at its address for the receipt of notices as herein set forth.
perturbation.explanation: The modifications give the Contractor sole discretion in dispute resolution. This alteration removes the neutrality of the arbitration process, potentially leading to biased outcomes that contradict fundamental principles of fairness and impartiality in arbitration law. The modified text also eliminates the arbitrator's power to award specific performance, rescission or injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Federal Arbitration Act (FAA) - Basic Principles of Fairness
perturbation.law_explanation: 9 U.S. Code § 10 outlines conditions under which an arbitration award can be vacated, including evident partiality or corruption in the arbitrator. By granting the Contractor sole discretion over dispute resolution, the modification creates inherent bias, undermining the FAA's principles of fairness and potentially rendering the arbitration agreement unenforceable. The exclusion of specific performance, rescission or injunctive relief limits the scope of resolution as defined by the court.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a clear bias by granting the Contractor sole discretion over dispute resolution. This directly contradicts the principle of impartiality, which is essential to arbitration law and is a ground for vacating an arbitration award under 9 U.S. Code § 10, as outlined in the Law Explanation. The modified text also eliminates the arbitrator's power to award specific performance, rescission or injunctive relief, limiting the scope of remedies available. The provided snippets highlight the structure and purpose of Title 9 - Arbitration, emphasizing the need for fair and unbiased proceedings, which is undermined by giving sole discretion to one party.
